name: Parque Natural de la Font Roja — Trail run circular to Menejador
name_original: Parque Natural de la Font Roja — Trail run circular al Menejador
region_intro: Jogging around Ibi offers diverse natural landscapes and an extensive network of trails within the Alicante province of Spain. The region is characterized by mountainous terrain, including the foothills of the Sierra de Onil and the larger Serra de Mariola mountain range, featuring valleys and ravines. Pine forests and Mediterranean scrubland cover much of the area, providing varied surfaces for running. This topography, with peaks and deep ravines, creates a dynamic environment for outdoor activities. Best jogging routes around Ibi The most popular jogging route is Parque Natural de la Font Roja — Trail run circular to Menejador, an 8.1 miles (13.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 2 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the natural park and ascends towards Menejador. Another top favourite among local runners is Menejador Summit – Elephant's Trunk Spring loop from Ibi, a difficult 12.9 miles (20.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain as it connects a prominent summit with a natural spring. Local runners also love the Ibi loop from Ibi, a 5.2 miles (8.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain around the town, often completed in about 55 minutes. Jogging around Ibi is defined by mountainous terrain, pine forests, and deep ravines. The network offers options for different ability levels, from shorter loops to challenging ascents. The routes in Ibi are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 500 runners have used komoot to explore Ibi's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Ibi?
A: Ibi offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 45 trails available for exploration. These routes cater to various preferences, from shorter loops to more challenging ascents through the region's mountainous terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Ibi?
A: The jogging trails around Ibi feature diverse terrain, ranging from mountainous areas within the Sierra de Onil and Serra de Mariola to flatter plains. You'll encounter pine forests, Mediterranean scrubland, and varied surfaces, including dirt paths and rocky sections, providing a dynamic and engaging running experience.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in Ibi?
A: Yes, while many routes in Ibi are considered difficult due to the mountainous landscape, there are a few easier options. The region offers 2 routes classified as easy and 18 as moderate, providing suitable choices for beginners or those looking for a less strenuous run. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31472409" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibi loop from Ibi&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 5.2-mile trail that takes about 55 minutes to complete, offering varied terrain around the town.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while jogging in Ibi?
A: Jogging in Ibi allows you to discover a rich natural and historical landscape. You can encounter interesting constructions like chapels, farmhouses, and ancient 'snow caves' (icehouses). The area is also known for its extensive network of caves and mountain passes. Notable highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4752696" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Canyo Ice Well&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4093328" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcoy–La Sagra Cave Greenway Trail&lt;/a&gt;, and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2604644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrasqueta Pass&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around Ibi?
A: Yes, Ibi offers several circular running routes that allow you to start and finish in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e872676590/parque-natural-de-la-font-roja-trail-run-circular-to-menejador" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de la Font Roja — Trail run circular to Menejador&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.1-mile route exploring the natural park. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31474056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Menejador Summit – Elephant's Trunk Spring loop from Ibi&lt;/a&gt;, a longer 12.9-mile path with significant elevation gain.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Ibi?
A: The running routes in Ibi are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average score of 4.4 stars from over 10 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse natural landscapes, the challenging yet rewarding ascents, and the opportunity to explore the mountainous terrain and pine forests.

Q: Are the jogging routes in Ibi suitable for families?
A: While many routes in Ibi involve significant elevation and are rated as difficult, there are options that families can enjoy. Look for the easier or moderate trails, particularly those that are shorter in distance. The region's natural parks also feature signposted paths, some of which are designed for more leisurely exploration, offering a chance to enjoy nature together.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Ibi?
A: The natural areas around Ibi, including the Serra de Mariola Natural Park, are generally welcoming to dogs, though specific regulations may apply in certain protected zones. It's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other visitors, and to ensure you clean up after them. The diverse trails offer plenty of space for you and your canine companion to enjoy a run.

Q: Is parking available near the trailheads in Ibi?
A: Yes, for many of the popular trailheads and natural parks around Ibi, parking facilities are available. For instance, if you plan to explore routes within the Serra de Mariola Natural Park or the Sierra de Onil, you'll typically find designated parking areas, making access convenient for runners.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Ibi?
A: Ibi's Mediterranean climate makes it suitable for jogging for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, ideal for longer runs. Summer can be hot, so early morning or late evening runs are recommended. Winter provides cooler, crisp conditions, perfect for invigorating runs, especially in the lower elevations.

Q: Are there any routes that connect to natural springs or water sources?
A: Yes, the region is known for its abundant water sources. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31474056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Menejador Summit – Elephant's Trunk Spring loop from Ibi&lt;/a&gt; is a notable route that connects a prominent summit with a natural spring, offering a refreshing point of interest during your run. The Serra de Mariola Natural Park, in particular, is renowned for its numerous fountains and springs.

Q: How long do the running routes in Ibi typically take to complete?
A: The duration of running routes in Ibi varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. Shorter loops, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31472409" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibi loop from Ibi&lt;/a&gt; (5.2 miles), can be completed in about 55 minutes. More challenging and longer routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e872676590/parque-natural-de-la-font-roja-trail-run-circular-to-menejador" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de la Font Roja — Trail run circular to Menejador&lt;/a&gt; (8.1 miles), typically take around 2 hours.
tips: With its summit at 1350 meters, Menejador offers incredible views in all directions. To reach its highest point, you have to pass a few meters from the forest box and follow a path through the trees until it ends. Returning by an alternative path you will have other views, all of them amazing.
